title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 9844 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Temporal Coupling Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Temporal Coupling Decision, keep Temporal Coupling Decision connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 5701 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Broker Fit Boundary trace for this workshop case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
